findings.implication: Applicant CV: Lists extensive research roles (Research Fellow, Advisor) and grants (NIHR, ESRC) demonstrating relevant experience and training in dementia and health services research.
Applicant CV: Provides a comprehensive publication record (20+ papers) in peer-reviewed journals and lists multiple grants and awards, indicating strong research outputs.

Applicant CV: Shows progression from PhD candidate to Research Fellow and Lead Applicant on grants, plus participation in leadership programs (Springboard, GW4 Crucible), indicating a trajectory towards research leadersh
Applicant CV: Demonstrates involvement in practice-based research through roles at NIHR Research Design Service and Dementia Centre, aligning with practitioner-academic career criteria.
Applicant CV: Evidence of prior successful projects (NIHR, ESRC grants) and a robust publication record supports the quality of previous research.
Applicant Research Background: Clearly articulates a research vision focused on unpaid carers' wellbeing and a career trajectory towards leadership in dementia research.
Applicant Research Background: States the Fellowship is a 'crucial step' to establish leadership and generate co-produced knowledge, explaining why this award is the right next step.
Applicant Research Background: Details 10 years of experience across all research stages, including RCTs, qualitative methods, and systematic reviews, demonstrating relevant experience and training.
Applicant Research Background: Lists 33 publications, h-index 13, and specific grants (NIHR, ESRC), providing strong evidence of impactful research outputs and outcomes.

Applicant Research Background: Shows increasing responsibility through leading trials (INFORM), securing funding, and serving as an Early Career Representative, indicating a trajectory towards research leadership.
Applicant Research Background: Describes work in adult social care research and co-production with unpaid carers, demonstrating commitment to a practitioner-academic career.
Applicant Research Background: Provides detailed evidence of prior successful projects (INFORM, MOVE) and a robust publication record, supporting the quality of previous research.
Applicant Research Background: Describes co-producing the Fellowship application with a team of eight unpaid carers, demonstrating integration of lived experience in research design.
Applicant Research Background: Mentions dissemination to diverse audiences including carers' organisations, blogs, and podcasts, showing commitment to maximising public benefit.
Plain English Summary of Research: Clearly outlines aims, background, methods, and dissemination in accessible language, supporting pr.1.
Plain English Summary of Research: Identifies a knowledge gap regarding support for unpaid carers' health and wellbeing, supporting pr.2.
Plain English Summary of Research: Describes a feasible three-stage design with specific sample sizes (100 carers, 30 consensus, focus groups), supporting pr.3.
Plain English Summary of Research: Explicitly details methods including Behaviour Change Wheel, needs assessment, consensus exercises, and focus groups, supporting pr.4.
Plain English Summary of Research: Highlights direct benefits for unpaid carers (physical activity, diet, time management) and dissemination to professionals, supporting pr.5.
Plain English Summary of Research: Describes unpaid carers as co-researchers involved in design, focus, and dissemination, supporting pr.6.
Plain English Summary of Research: Focuses on unpaid carers across England and Scotland, with co-production by carers themselves, supporting pr.9.
Plain English Summary of Research: Confirms unpaid carers co-produced the application and will act as co-researchers, reinforcing wp.1.
Plain English Summary of Research: Details dissemination via podcasts, articles, and illustrators to reach carers and professionals, reinforcing wp.3.
Scientific Abstract: Clearly outlines research question, background, aims, methods, and dissemination in accessible language, supporting pr.1.
Scientific Abstract: Identifies an evidence gap in literature regarding unpaid carers' health and plans a systematic review, supporting pr.2.
Scientific Abstract: Describes a feasible three-stage design with specific timelines and sample sizes (100 carers, 30 consensus), supporting pr.3.
Scientific Abstract: Explicitly details mixed methods including survey, systematic review, Delphi, and focus groups aligned with Behaviour Change Wheel, supporting pr.4.
Scientific Abstract: Highlights direct benefits for unpaid carers' health and wellbeing and plans diverse dissemination, supporting pr.5.
Scientific Abstract: Describes unpaid carers co-producing the research and participating in all stages as core team members, supporting pr.6.
Scientific Abstract: Focuses on unpaid carers across England with co-production by carers themselves, supporting pr.9.
Scientific Abstract: Confirms eight unpaid carers co-produced the research and will be part of the core team, supporting wp.1.
Scientific Abstract: Details dissemination via podcasts, webinars, and articles to reach carers and professionals, supporting wp.3.
Detailed Research Plan: Explicitly identifies knowledge gaps regarding physical activity, diet, and time management for carers, citing weak existing evidence and lack of studies, supporting pr.2.
Detailed Research Plan: Outlines a feasible 44-month three-stage design with specific sample sizes (100-500 survey, 30 Delphi) and clear milestones, supporting pr.3.
Detailed Research Plan: Provides explicit methodological descriptions including MRC guidance, Behaviour Change Wheel, Delphi exercises, and focus groups with specific analysis plans, supporting pr.4.
Detailed Research Plan: Highlights societal impact by addressing high costs of dementia care and carer health issues, with plans for documentaries and policy engagement, supporting pr.5.
Detailed Research Plan: Demonstrates deep engagement with unpaid carers as co-researchers involved in design, recruitment, and dissemination, supporting pr.6.
Detailed Research Plan: Justifies resources by requesting a 50% FTE research associate and training budget to support project management and co-researcher activities, supporting pr.7.
Detailed Research Plan: Details inclusive strategies including EDI data collection, recruitment from diverse ethnic/LGBTQ+ groups, translation of documents, and interpreter support, supporting pr.9.
Detailed Research Plan: Confirms co-production with eight unpaid carers who identified research concerns and will participate in Delphi exercises and focus groups, supporting wp.1.
Detailed Research Plan: Describes a robust dissemination strategy including a documentary, blogs, and engagement with policy makers to maximize public benefit, supporting wp.3.

Detailed Research Plan: Explicitly targets seldom-heard groups including ethnic minorities, LGBTQ+ communities, and those with language barriers through specific recruitment partnerships, supporting wp.4.
Detailed Research Plan: Ensures inclusivity across the lifecycle via translated materials, flexible participation modes, and co-researcher involvement in all stages, supporting wp.5.
Detailed Research Plan: Allocates resources for replacement costs for carers, interpreters, and training for co-researchers, supporting wp.6.
Detailed Research Plan: Mentions support from Brighton and Sussex Clinical Trials Unit for data management and statistics, indicating appropriate organizational support, supporting ss.3.

Patient & Public Involvement: Unpaid carers co-produced the application, identified goals, and provided feedback on methods and dissemination, demonstrating deep integration of lived experience in research design.

Patient & Public Involvement: The co-researcher team includes diverse characteristics such as ethnicity, LGBTQ+ identity, language background, and socioeconomic status, supporting diversity and inclusivity of the researc

Patient & Public Involvement: The team includes individuals from minority backgrounds, and a new co-researcher will be specifically recruited from a minority background to further diversify voices, addressing representat

Patient & Public Involvement: Co-researchers are involved in all stages from design to dissemination, with flexible meeting formats and adaptive commitment levels to accommodate fluctuating health, ensuring inclusivity a
Patient & Public Involvement: The plan includes budgeted payments for attendance, internet costs, carer replacement costs, and training, providing a costed and resourced plan for research inclusion.

Training & Development and Research Support: Provides a comprehensive, structured training plan covering Behaviour Change, Co-production, EDI, Impact, Leadership, and Software skills, directly supporting td.1.

Training & Development and Research Support: Explicitly identifies personal skill gaps in conflict resolution, facilitation, and Implementation Science, mapping specific courses to address these needs, supporting td.2.

Training & Development and Research Support: Links training in Leadership, Implementation Science, and Economic Evaluation to career goals of becoming an independent research leader and influencing policy, supporting td.
Training & Development and Research Support: Highlights CHSS's track record in health/social care research, NIHR grants, and strong partnerships with ARC KSS and local authorities, supporting ss.1.

Training & Development and Research Support: Details four supervisors with specific expertise (co-production, behavioural science, dementia, public health) and structured meeting schedules, supporting ss.2.
Training & Development and Research Support: Describes institutional commitment to EDI via Athena Swan, Race Equality Charter, and specific training for selection panels, supporting ss.4.

SUMMARY BUDGET: Provides detailed justifications linking staff FTE, PPI payments, and training costs directly to project tasks like co-production, data management, and dissemination, strongly supporting pr.7.
SUMMARY BUDGET: Explicitly budgets for PPI attendance, training, carer replacement, and travel, demonstrating a fully costed and resourced plan for research inclusion, supporting wp.6.

SUMMARY BUDGET: Allocates funds for document translation into three languages and interpreter services, providing concrete evidence of resources dedicated to inclusive research design, supporting pr.9.

SUMMARY BUDGET: Details specific, high-value training courses (Behaviour Change Wheel, Intervention Mapping, Co-production) with clear costs and timelines, supporting the quality and relevance of proposed training, td.1.
SUMMARY BUDGET: Justifies training in conflict resolution and facilitation as identified gaps from co-producing the application, and links impact training to career goals, supporting td.2.
SUMMARY BUDGET: Budgets for shared staff from Brighton and Sussex CTU for data management and statistics, confirming organizational infrastructure support, supporting ss.3.
Plain English NLP Analysis: NLP metrics show strong readability (Flesch-Kincaid 7.36, 0% long sentences) and low jargon density (2.35%), supporting the quality of the Plain English Summary.

Application Form Analysis: Confirms presence of Plain English Summary (610 words) and Scientific Abstract (519 words) with low lexical overlap (0.376), indicating distinct content rather than simple duplication, supporti

Application Form Analysis: Identifies 224 table-like budget lines and high lexical overlap (0.279-0.314) between budget and research/training sections, suggesting strong integration of costs with project scope, supportin

Application Form Analysis: Reports 58 transition phrases and logical section ordering from context to budget, indicating coherent flow and structure in the evidence review and proposal, supporting pr.2.
Application Form Analysis: Shows a substantial Detailed Research Plan (5076 words) relative to other sections, suggesting adequate scope and detail for the proposed award, supporting pr.3.

Application Form Analysis: Confirms a dedicated Patient & Public Involvement section (767 words) with significant lexical overlap with the budget (0.258), indicating PPI is integrated into resource planning, supporting p

Application Form Analysis: Identifies a substantial Training & Development section (3625 words) with high lexical overlap with the budget (0.314), suggesting detailed and costed training plans, supporting td.1.
